sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: Rollins Q4 2025 misses forecasts, stock drops
feed.summary: Rollins Inc. reported Q4 2025 earnings per share of $0.25 and revenue of $913 million, missing forecasts of $0.26 and $926.81 million, respectively. The company's stock subsequently dropped significantly by 16.87% in premarket trading. Despite the Q4 miss, Rollins recorded full-year revenue growth of 11% for 2025 and projects continued organic growth of 7-8% in 2026, driven by strategic initiatives and robust recurring services.

feed.title: Campbell’s to shutter Cape Cod potato chip plant
feed.summary: Campbell’s plans to close its Cape Cod potato chip plant in Hyannis, Massachusetts, by April, moving production to other facilities to improve efficiency. This facility is the smallest in Campbell's network for potato chips and accounts for only 4% of the total annual volume of Cape Cod Chips. The closure will impact 49 employees and is part of Campbell's broader strategy to consolidate operations and invest in more efficient plants, similar to actions taken by other major food companies.

feed.title: Expansion Into Data Center Market Is Poised to Drive Generac's Medium-Term Sales Growth
feed.summary: Generac is transitioning into an energy technology solutions company, with its expansion into the data center market expected to drive medium-term sales growth. Analysts express growing confidence in this data center opportunity, noting its importance for durability and offsetting weaker power outage environments.

feed.title: Edison International Stock: Analyst Estimates & Ratings
feed.summary: Edison International (EIX) has outperformed the broader market and the utilities sector over the past year and in 2026, with its stock growing 32% and 11.7% respectively. The company reported better-than-expected Q3 2025 earnings, with revenue surpassing Street expectations and adjusted EPS beating estimates. Analysts project a significant 24.5% year-over-year surge in adjusted EPS for the fiscal year ending December 2025, and the stock holds a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating.

feed.title: SLB wins offshore drilling pacts for giant Asia gas development
feed.summary: Mubadala Energy has awarded offshore drilling contracts to SLB for the Tangkulo field development in Indonesian waters. This agreement positions SLB as a key contractor for a significant gas development project in Asia. The article highlights Upstream's role as a trusted source for news and analysis in the energy industry, including the transition to cleaner energy.

feed.title: Deutsche Bank Raises Price Target on Hilton Worldwide Holdings to $332 From $299, Keeps Hold Rating
feed.summary: Deutsche Bank has increased its price target for Hilton Worldwide Holdings (HLT) to $332 from $299, while maintaining a Hold rating on the stock. This adjustment comes amidst similar moves by other financial institutions, with multiple analysts revising their price targets for Hilton, generally upward, following recent financial reports.

feed.title: CarMax names Keith Barr as chief executive officer
feed.summary: CarMax Inc. has appointed Keith Barr as its new President and Chief Executive Officer, effective March 16, 2026. Barr previously served as CEO of InterContinental Hotels Group and is currently on the board of MGM Resorts. This appointment follows Bill Nash's stepping down in November 2025, with current interim CEO David McCreight returning to his role as an independent director and Tom Folliard remaining as interim executive chair until June 2026.

feed.title: CarMax names Keith Barr as chief executive officer
feed.summary: CarMax Inc. has appointed Keith Barr as its new President and Chief Executive Officer, effective March 16, 2026, succeeding Bill Nash. Barr previously served as CEO of InterContinental Hotels Group and is currently on the board of MGM Resorts. David McCreight, interim president and CEO, will return to his role as an independent director, while Tom Folliard will remain interim executive chair until June 2026.

feed.title: The Great Distortion: 1,500 Stocks Vs. The Magnificent 7 In Norway's Massive U.S. Equity Portfolio
feed.summary: Norway's Government Pension Fund Global, the world's largest sovereign wealth fund, holds 1,577 U.S. stocks, yet nearly one-third of its $935 billion U.S. equity portfolio is concentrated in just seven "Magnificent 7" companies. This massive concentration, led by Nvidia, Apple, and Microsoft, highlights a growing market distortion where institutional capital heavily favors a few mega-cap tech giants. The analysis suggests that while diversification may appear broad, market performance increasingly depends on these powerful few stocks.

feed.title: Topeka and Shawnee County OK $530K in incentives for these 2 companies
feed.summary: The Joint Economic Development Organization (JEDO) board of Topeka and Shawnee County has approved $530,000 in incentives for two local manufacturing companies: J.M. Smucker Co. and HF Rubber. J.M. Smucker Co. will receive $383,000 for a $20.5 million expansion focused on employee facilities, while HF Rubber will get $147,000 for a $1.6 million expansion projected to create 19 new jobs. These investments are expected to strengthen Topeka's manufacturing sector and generate significant economic impact for the region.

feed.title: Citigroup Adjusts Price Target on AvalonBay Communities to $198 From $212, Maintains Neutral Rating
feed.summary: Citigroup has revised its price target for AvalonBay Communities (NYSE: AVB) down to $198 from $212 while keeping a Neutral rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects a change in the bank's valuation perspective for the real estate investment trust specializing in apartment communities. The article also lists other recent analyst adjustments for AvalonBay, with varying price targets and ratings.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Lowers Price Target on Martin Marietta Materials (MLM)
feed.summary: Wells Fargo maintained an "Equal-Weight" rating on Martin Marietta Materials (MLM) but lowered its price target from $631 to $612, a 3.01% decrease. This adjustment reflects the firm's updated valuation metrics and market conditions. Despite the lowered target, the average target price from 26 analysts for MLM is $683.80, suggesting an upside, while GuruFocus estimates a GF Value of $557.27, indicating a downside.

feed.title: BNP Paribas Adjusts Price Target on Freeport-McMoRan to $75 From $56, Maintains Outperform Rating
feed.summary: BNP Paribas has raised its price target for Freeport-McMoRan (FCX) to $75 from $56, while reiterating an Outperform rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects a more optimistic outlook by the firm on the mining company. Freeport-McMoRan specializes in copper, gold, and molybdenum, with significant operations globally.

feed.title: FedEx targets 14% profit growth in 4-year strategy
feed.summary: FedEx has announced a four-year strategy targeting a 14% growth in adjusted operating profit by 2029, aiming for $98 billion in annual revenue. The logistics company plans to achieve this through prioritizing high-margin segments, scaling digital and AI capabilities, transforming its shipping network, and making efficiency gains permanent. FedEx also expects an 8% operating margin and will keep capital expenditures low, including capping aircraft spending at $1 billion through 2029.

feed.title: DexCom earnings up next: Can G7 rollout offset Abbott competition? By Investing.com
feed.summary: DexCom is set to release its fourth-quarter earnings, with investors focusing on the impact of its new G7 15-day sensor rollout amidst increasing competition from Abbott Laboratories. Analysts expect DexCom to report earnings of $0.6459 per share on revenue of $1.24 billion. The company faces significant rivalry in the continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) market, and the success of the G7 launch and potential Medicare coverage expansion will be crucial in offsetting competitive pressures.

feed.title: Stephens Keeps an Equal Weight Rating on Global Payments Inc. (GPN)
feed.summary: Stephens has maintained an Equal Weight rating on Global Payments Inc. (GPN) but lowered its price target from $95 to $90, citing a difficult 2025 for the company and the broader payments industry. Despite this, Stephens anticipates a positive outlook for the industry in 2026. Cantor Fitzgerald also initiated coverage with a neutral rating and an $80 price target, noting that GPN's longer-term organic growth algorithm is expected to be lower due to a divestment and refresh plan.

feed.title: Public Storage earnings in focus after wave of analyst downgrades By Investing.com
feed.summary: Public Storage (PSA) is scheduled to release its fourth-quarter earnings, with analysts anticipating $2.50 per share in earnings and $1.22 billion in revenue. The company faces increased scrutiny following several analyst downgrades, highlighting investor concerns about its ability to navigate a shift from growth to "measured stability" in the self-storage industry. Investors will be closely watching management's outlook on future growth, same-store performance, and the potential impact of housing market trends.

feed.title: Iron Mountain stock hits 52-week high at $108.18
feed.summary: Iron Mountain (IRM) stock recently reached a 52-week high of $108.18, representing a 14.14% increase over the past year. This achievement highlights strong investor confidence and the company's strategic growth initiatives. The company also reported a significant 17% year-over-year revenue increase in Q4 2025, reaching $1.84 billion, and an EPS of $0.61, surpassing analyst expectations.

feed.title: Prologis stock hits 52-week high at $141.86 By Investing.com
feed.summary: Prologis Inc. (PLD) stock recently hit a 52-week high of $141.86, just above its current trading price of $140.95, reflecting a significant positive trend with a 35.7% return over the past six months and a 20.95% increase over the last year. Despite trading at a high P/E ratio of 40.4, the company maintains a strong market position with a $134.6 billion market capitalization and a "GOOD" financial health score, further bolstered by a 2.89% dividend yield and robust Q4 2025 earnings which surpassed analyst expectations. Analyst firms like Truist Securities, Freedom Capital Markets, BMO Capital, and Evercore ISI have adjusted their price targets and ratings following these strong results, indicating continued confidence in the logistics real estate giant's resilience and growth potential.

feed.title: Ross Stores Inc stock hits all-time high at $195.03 By Investing.com
feed.summary: Ross Stores Inc. (ROST) stock has reached a new all-time high of $195.03, marking a significant 39.36% increase over the past year. This performance highlights strong investor confidence and the company's solid financial health, bolstered by strategic initiatives and operational success. Recent positive news includes strong third-quarter earnings, a board chair succession plan, and raised price targets from analysts at TD Cowen and BofA Securities.

feed.title: NextEra Energy's Stock Recovery Potential Emerges Amid AI Demand
feed.summary: NextEra Energy is well-positioned for significant growth due to the surging electricity demand driven by artificial intelligence, with data centers projected to consume substantial power. The company stands to benefit from substantial tax credits for its extensive wind and solar projects, cementing its leadership in clean energy. Furthermore, NextEra Energy offers attractive dividend growth potential with a 31-year streak of increases and projected hikes, making it appealing to income investors as interest rates decline.

feed.title: The Hershey Company’s ‘Take the Taste With You’ platform drives performance gains across global travel retail
feed.summary: The Hershey Company's "Take the Taste With You" platform is successfully driving engagement and sales in global travel retail by converting its new corporate branding into stronger traveler interaction across major airport hubs. The platform uses immersive displays, digital touchpoints, and interactive activations tailored to local markets, resulting in significant performance gains like increased sell-out in Paris and Abu Dhabi. Hershey plans further expansion and high-visibility activations in 2026 to continue this positive momentum.

feed.title: Brown & Brown sees rate conditions shifting in favour of prepared buyers
feed.summary: Brown & Brown's 2026 Market Trends report indicates a shift in rate conditions benefiting buyers who are well-prepared for renewals and maintain strong claims performance. While overall market capacity has increased, conditions vary by segment, with commercial insurance seeing heightened competition but continued challenges in catastrophe-prone areas and specific industries. The report also highlights evolving dynamics in employee benefits and personal insurance markets due to climate volatility and increased claims severity.

feed.title: Snap-On stock reaches all-time high at $389.31 By Investing.com
feed.summary: Snap-On Inc. (NYSE:SNA) has achieved a new all-time high stock price of $389.31, representing a 17.39% increase over the past year. The company boasts robust gross profit margins of 51.73% and an impressive streak of 55 consecutive years of dividend payments, holding more cash than debt. Despite surpassing Q4 2025 EPS expectations, the company missed revenue forecasts, raising some investor concerns regarding operating margin contraction.

feed.title: Pinnacle West Capital Corp stock hits 52-week high at 96.75 USD
feed.summary: Pinnacle West Capital Corp (PNW) has reached a new 52-week high of $96.75, with its current price at $96.95, reflecting a 12.23% total return over the past year. The utility company offers a dividend yield of 3.79% and a P/E ratio of 19.42, maintaining dividend payments for 34 consecutive years. However, KeyBanc recently downgraded the stock to Sector Weight due to anticipated delays in benefits from the company's Formula Rate Plan transition, with substantial improvements not expected until 2028.

feed.title: Globant SA stock hits 52-week low at 54.13 USD

Despite its profitability and solid current ratio, investor concerns persist, particularly within the tech sector. Analysts view the stock as undervalued, with price targets ranging from $68 to $150, and are closely watching strategic adjustments and the upcoming earnings report.

feed.title: Avery Dennison stock hits 52-week high at 196.48 USD

This comes despite a recent Q4 2025 revenue shortfall, though adjusted EPS exceeded forecasts, and BMO Capital raised its price target for AVY, citing expected earnings growth in 2026 and 2027.

feed.title: Tapestry Inc stock hits all-time high at 158.28 USD By Investing.com
feed.summary: Tapestry Inc (TPR) has reached an all-time high of $158.28 USD, trading just below its 52-week high, with a P/E ratio of 61.37 suggesting it might be overvalued. The luxury retailer has seen an 84.62% increase in stock value over the past year, boasts impressive gross profit margins of 75.98%, and has consistently paid dividends for 18 years. This growth follows strong Q2 fiscal year 2026 results, surpassing analyst expectations for EPS and revenue, leading to raised price targets from Wells Fargo and UBS.

feed.title: Packaging Corp of America stock hits 52-week high at $247.66
feed.summary: Packaging Corp of America (PKG) stock reached a 52-week high of $249.11, demonstrating strong performance with a 22.49% total return over the last year and consistent dividend payments. Despite a recent Q4 2025 earnings miss, the company maintains investor confidence through strategic investments. Analysts will closely monitor PKG, as InvestingPro suggests it may be trading above its Fair Value and in overbought territory.

feed.title: Virginia student spotlights Dr. Gladys West, whose work was foundational to GPS
feed.summary: Dominion Energy and the Library of Virginia have announced the 2026 "Strong Men & Women in Virginia History" honorees and student contest finalists, celebrating African American leaders' contributions. Among the recognized, high school student Vrinda Shah won the creative contest for designing a website highlighting Dr. Gladys Mae West, whose work was crucial to GPS development. This annual program aims to educate Virginians on the significant impact of these individuals on the Commonwealth's history and future.

feed.title: JPMorgan Adjusts Northern Trust Price Target to $153.50 From $145.50, Maintains Neutral Rating
feed.summary: JPMorgan has increased its price target for Northern Trust (NTRS) to $153.50 from $145.50, while maintaining a Neutral rating on the stock. This adjustment comes amidst other analyst actions on Northern Trust, reflecting diverse perspectives on the company's valuation following recent financial reports and conferences. The article also provides a brief overview of Northern Trust's business, highlighting its investment services and wealth management segments.

feed.title: Geo Group: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Geo Group Inc. (GEO) reported a fourth-quarter profit of $31.8 million, or 23 cents per share, aligning with Wall Street expectations after adjustments. The private prison operator's revenue for the quarter was $707.7 million, exceeding analyst forecasts. For the full year, Geo Group posted a profit of $254.4 million and projected future revenues between $680 million and $690 million for the current quarter.

feed.title: Gabelli Equity Trust 10% Distribution Policy Reaffirmed and Declared First Quarter Distribution of $0.15 Per Share
feed.summary: The Gabelli Equity Trust Inc. (NYSE:GAB) reaffirmed its 10% distribution policy and declared a $0.15 per share cash distribution payable on March 24, 2026. The Fund aims to pay a minimum annual distribution of 10% of its average net asset value, with the current distribution including primarily a return of capital. Shareholders are advised that the distribution rate should not be viewed as the dividend yield or total return.

feed.title: Albemarle (ALB) Maintains Neutral Rating; Price Target Raised by Mizuho | ALB Stock News
feed.summary: Mizuho has raised its price target for Albemarle (ALB) to $185, while maintaining a Neutral rating. This adjustment comes amidst a series of upward price target revisions from various analysts, including Jefferies, JP Morgan, Wells Fargo, Truist Securities, and Oppenheimer, indicating an optimistic market outlook for the lithium producer. Despite the raised price targets, GuruFocus estimates a potential downside due to the GF Value significantly differing from the current stock price.

feed.title: Uber Eats launches agentic AI-based grocery list tool
feed.summary: Uber Eats has introduced Cart Assistant, an agentic AI-based grocery list tool aimed at simplifying the shopping experience. This new beta feature allows users to create grocery lists by typing or uploading images, automatically checking item availability and prices at selected stores. The tool personalizes suggestions based on past orders and is launching in various large-chain grocery and drugstores across the U.S.

feed.title: PPG Receives Analyst Rating Update: Mizuho Raises Price Target
feed.summary: Mizuho analyst John Roberts has maintained an "Outperform" rating for PPG Industries and increased its price target from $125.00 to $140.00, reflecting positive sentiment. This update comes alongside similar positive adjustments from other major analysts like Wells Fargo, RBC Capital, Goldman Sachs, UBS, and JP Morgan in late January 2026. PPG, a global producer of coatings, has received an average brokerage recommendation of 2.4, indicating an "Outperform" status, and GuruFocus estimates a GF Value of $142.17, suggesting an 8.5% upside.

feed.title: Howmet Aerospace jumps to 52-week high on higher jet demand (HWM:NYSE)
feed.summary: Howmet Aerospace (HWM) shares rose over 10% to a 52-week high after reporting fourth-quarter results that surpassed Wall Street expectations. The company's strong performance was driven by growth in commercial aerospace, defense, and gas turbines. Howmet Aerospace also provided an optimistic 2026 outlook, with guidance for revenue, adjusted EPS, and free cash flow exceeding analyst forecasts.

feed.title: Host Hotels & Resorts stock hits 52-week high at $20.14 By Investing.com

feed.title: AES Indiana’s new solar energy center in Petersburg now online
feed.summary: AES Indiana has activated a new 200-megawatt solar energy center in Pike County, marking a significant step in the state's transition to renewable energy. This facility, built in partnership with Orion Renewable Energy Group, is expected to power over 37,000 homes annually and is part of AES Indiana's larger plan to add 1,400 megawatts of renewable energy by 2027. The project represents a $200 million investment and highlights a growing trend in Indiana's energy sector.

feed.title: Global Payments (NYSE:GPN) Given New $90.00 Price Target at JPMorgan Chase & Co.
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. has lowered its price target for Global Payments (NYSE:GPN) from $95.00 to $90.00, maintaining a "neutral" rating on the stock, implying a potential upside of 25.19%. Other analysts have also adjusted their ratings, with the company currently holding an average "Hold" rating and a consensus target price of $97.59. Insider activity includes a significant stock sale by David Lawrence Green and a substantial purchase by Director Robert H. B. Baldwin, Jr., while institutional investors like Aviva PLC and Private Management Group Inc. have increased their holdings.

feed.title: Equity Residential (NYSE:EQR) Price Target Raised to $71.00 at The Goldman Sachs Group
feed.summary: The Goldman Sachs Group has increased its price target for Equity Residential (NYSE:EQR) to $71.00 from $69.00, maintaining a "neutral" rating, indicating a potential upside of 9.33%. Despite this, analyst sentiment remains mixed, with an average price target of $69.68 among numerous firms. Recent Q4 earnings slightly missed estimates, and the company issued Q1 and FY2026 EPS guidance, while significant insider selling has occurred, potentially signaling short-term downward pressure.

feed.title: TD Cowen Raises Becton, Dickinson and Company (NYSE:BDX) Price Target to $189.00
feed.summary: TD Cowen has increased its price target for Becton, Dickinson and Company (NYSE:BDX) to $189 from $183, while reiterating a "hold" rating. This adjustment follows Becton, Dickinson beating its Q1 earnings and revenue estimates, reporting $2.91 EPS against an expected $2.81, and revenues of $5.25 billion. The company also announced a modest share buyback of $10 million and tender offers to repurchase up to $1.6 billion in debt, reflecting active capital management.

feed.title: BMO Capital Markets Lowers T. Rowe Price Group (NASDAQ:TROW) Price Target to $104.00
feed.summary: BMO Capital Markets has reduced its price target for T. Rowe Price Group (NASDAQ:TROW) to $104.00 from $110.00, while maintaining a "market perform" rating. Other firms like Evercore, Morgan Stanley, JPMorgan, and Goldman Sachs have also recently lowered their price targets for the asset manager. The company recently reported quarterly earnings of $2.44 EPS, missing consensus estimates by $0.03, and revenue of $1.93 billion, which was up 6% year-over-year.

feed.title: Western Digital stock hits all-time high at 297.8 USD By Investing.com
feed.summary: Western Digital Corporation's stock reached an all-time high of $302.73, signifying a 440.52% return over the past year and outperforming the broader technology sector. The company has a market capitalization exceeding $101 billion and has seen 17 analysts revise earnings estimates upward. While InvestingPro analysis suggests the stock is overvalued relative to its Fair Value, its financial health is rated "GREAT" and the company is strategically focusing on innovation in data storage, particularly for AI needs.

feed.title: Fund Update: New $47.5M $LRCX stock position opened by CINCINNATI FINANCIAL CORP
feed.summary: CINCINNATI FINANCIAL CORP has opened a new $47.5 million position in Lam Research Corporation ($LRCX) stock, as revealed in a recent SEC 13F filing for the 12-31-2025 report period. This move comes amidst significant institutional investor activity in $LRCX, with 1,073 institutions adding shares and 945 decreasing positions in the last quarter. Notably, insiders have primarily been selling $LRCX stock, while Congressional members have also engaged in both purchases and sales.

feed.title: SIG Maintains Wynn Resorts(WYNN.US) With Buy Rating, Raises Target Price to $133
feed.summary: SIG analyst Joseph Stauff has reiterated a "buy" rating for Wynn Resorts (WYNN.US) and increased the target price from $122 to $133. According to TipRanks data, Stauff has a 54.0% success rate and an average return of 12.4% over the past year.
